name: skill-vetter
tagline: Popular ClawHub skill (1.3k★): a security-first checklist for vetting other skills before install. Meta — evaluated by a grader.
verdict: Security-clean and its listed attack patterns are documentation, not danger. Its blind spot: it never says when NOT to use it, so it could misfire on plain code review instead of security vetting. Also assumes jq is installed without checking.

highlights.summary: No 'NOT for' / out-of-scope guidance — could misfire on general code review.
jq is invoked with no requirements note or availability check.
Lists attack patterns (curl|bash, ~/.ssh, base64) as things to DETECT — correctly read as detection material, not an attack; layer-1 flags 3, the rubric de-escalates.
